content.claim: Hungarian school funding structure includes per-capita normative funding multipliers (2x to 3x) for SNI students, potentially creating fiscal incentive for enrollment-declining schools to classify students as SNI to offset revenue loss and avoid teacher dismissal or closure
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Source is course material, not peer-reviewed or official policy document; institutional attribution and date of material creation are unclear
Hypothesis is not independently verified by reference to statute, budget data, or administrative records of classification patterns
No evidence of scale, frequency, or current prevalence of this incentive effect in practice
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
